criteria.question: Will the Lender accept that the Applicant has been Electronically Identified by the Broker removing the need for Certified ID?
criteria.notes: No - Accord will attempt to electronically identify the applicants.

criteria.question: Will the lender consider an application where the accountant holds a non-standard qualification? If not, what are the recognised qualifications required for an Accountant's work to be recognised by the Lender?
criteria.notes: Institute of Chartered Accountants (FCA/ACA) Institute of Chartered Accountants (Scotland) - (FCAS/ACAS) Association of Chartered Certified Accountants (ACCA/FCCA) Chartered Institute of Public Finance Accountants (CIPFA) Institute of Company Accountants (ASCA/FSCA) Association of Authorised Public Accountants (FAPA/AAPA) Association of International Accountants (FIA/AIA) Chartered Institute of Management Accountants (CIMA) Institute of Chartered Accountants of Ireland (ICAI) Institute of Chartered Accountants Scotland (ICAS).

criteria.question: Is the Lender happy to accept an application without the need for an Accountant's Letter or Reference?
criteria.notes: If an applicant hasn't got an accountant, Accord will accept the last 3 years SA302 documents and supporting Tax Year Overviews along with the latest personal and business bank statement.

criteria.question: Will the Lender take an Accountant's Projection into consideration when assessing Affordability?
criteria.notes: Not acceptable - projection are used to show sustainability of income and not for affordability.

criteria.question: Is an Accountant's Letter or Reference accepted by the Lender in place of either Accounts or an SA302?
criteria.notes: Accountants reference accepted if accountant is on list of recognised accountants with latest personal and business bank statement. In the absence of recognised qualification would also require last three years SA302's

criteria.criteria_name: Add Fees to Loan above Max LTV
tags: add arrangement fee take above ltv
add arrangement fee take over ltv
add arrangement fees take over ltv
criteria.question: Will the Lender allow the Applicant to add any Arrangement or Booking Fees to the Loan when by doing this it takes them above either the LTV for their chosen product or the Lender's LTV limits?
criteria.notes: Product fees can be added above the LTV restriction providing it meets affordability with the fees added.

criteria.question: Is it possible to add Fees associated with the mortgage to the loan? If so what is acceptable?
criteria.notes: Where the applicant chooses to add the Product Fee to the mortgage at completion, Accord will not include this in the LTV calculation. Subject to the loan still being affordable with the fee included.

criteria.question: Is Additional Borrowing to the main loan permitted by the Lender?
criteria.notes: Applicants (those currently on a Prime product) must have had their product for at least 6 months and have maintained full mortgage payments on time during the term. Credit Repair applicants (those currently on a Credit Repair product) must have a minimum 24 months track record before an additional loan can be considered and all payments within the last 24 months must have been made on time and in full. Max LTV is 85%.

criteria.question: Are there any Hints, Tips or important points to note that are useful when using the Lender's Affordability Calculator? If so, what are these?
criteria.notes: Please note in the over 17's section that an applicant can not be self financing from an applicant. An applicant will always be classed as dependent. Accords Affordability Assessment takes into consideration that utility bills for new builds are typically lower and so will automatically apply a discount to the clients expenditure allowing them to borrow more

criteria.criteria_name: Specialist Range or Criteria for Professionals
Spray Foam Insulation Underside of Roof

criteria.question: Is Statutory Sick Pay an acceptable source of income for Affordability Assessment? If so, what is the policy on this?
criteria.notes: Further information would need to provided to anticipated return to work

criteria.question: Are Steel Framed Properties considered to be acceptable security to the Lender?
criteria.notes: Require construction name type and will require a intrusive structural engineers report on the steel frame if built pre 1980. Also subject to valuers comments.

criteria.question: Is a Stipend an acceptable source of income for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: No

criteria.criteria_name: Stocks & Shares as an Interest Only Repayment Vehicle
criteria.question: Will the Lender accept Stocks & Shares as an acceptable Repayment Strategy on an Interest Only Mortgage?
criteria.notes: Acceptable - needs to be sterling UK based e and cover 100% of the mortgage.

criteria.question: Will the lender ignore Student Loan deductions in the assessment of affordability? What is the policy on this?
criteria.notes: No. Student loan would need to be keyed as a commitment.

criteria.question: Will the lender consider an applicant who does not have indefinite leave to remain but who does have a Student Visa? If so, what is the policy on this?
criteria.notes: Customers whose nationality is not British or Irish must provide evidence that they have either indefinite leave to remain or right to reside, via a passport (showing stamp), EU settlement scheme share code or confirmation from the Home Office

criteria.question: Are Studio Flats or Studio Apartments considered to be acceptable security to the Lender?
criteria.notes: Not usually acceptable. If very high demand may be considered.

criteria.criteria_name: Sub-Sales and Reassignment of Sale Contracts
criteria.question: Will the Lender consider an Application where there is a Sub-Sale and/or a Reassignment of the Sale Contract?
criteria.notes: Sub sales not acceptable.

criteria.question: Are Subcontractors an acceptable form of employment and source of income for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: Refer

criteria.question: Does the Lender have specific policy about being able to lend to Teachers? i.e. Probationary Periods, Supply Teachers, Relocating prior to commencement of appointment etc.
criteria.notes: We disregard probationary periods. If moving to a new school/position, if the job is a permanent position, we can accept new jobs within 3 months of the start date. For fixed term contracts at least 12 months of a contract remaining; or Where less than 12 months remaining (minimum original length of 12 months), a 2 year track record of income from the contract work will be required.

criteria.question: Is Temporary Work or Temporary Employment an acceptable source of employment and income for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: Considered on appeal if they have a 3 year history of working on a temporary contract and case not solely reliant on this income.

criteria.question: Will the lender consider an applicant who does not have indefinite leave to remain but who does have a Temporary Work - International Agreement Visa? If so, what is the policy on this?
criteria.notes: Customers whose nationality is not British or Irish must provide evidence that they have either indefinite leave to remain or right to reside, via a passport (showing stamp), EU settlement scheme share code or confirmation from the Home Office

criteria.question: What Tenures are acceptable to the Lender? What is the specific policy detail on these?
criteria.notes: Freehold, Commonhold, Heritable and Leasehold properties are acceptable. For leasehold/feudal properties the following must apply: There should be at least 85 years unexpired lease remaining at the start of the mortgage application. Ground rent and service charge costs to be included in the affordability calculation.

criteria.criteria_name: Third Party Income from Person Not Named on Mortgage
criteria.question: Is a Third Party Income, from a Spouse, Partner, Parents etc. who is not going to be an Applicant on the Mortgage, an acceptable source of income for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: No

criteria.question: Are Timber Framed Properties considered to be acceptable security to the Lender?
criteria.notes: Acceptable if the property has brick block stone or render exterior (something not flammable) If timber frame built pre 1965 not acceptable

tags: foreign nationals time remaining on visa
non eu time remaining on visa
criteria.question: Will the lender consider an applicant without placing a minimum period of time that must be remaining an applicant's Visa?
criteria.notes: Applicants must be resident in the uk at the time of application. Without indefinite leave to remain in the uk the max ltv is 75%. Applicants must have leave to remain in the uk to be considered for a mortgage application. We can accept temporary leave to remain however would be subject to underwriters approval if only a limited time remaining on the visa.

criteria.criteria_name: Total Aggregate Borrowing on Portfolio with Lender
tags: resi and btl with same lender
residential and buy to let with same lender
btl and resi with same lender
buy to let and residential with same lender
criteria.question: Will the lender consider an application without any Exposure Limits within their own Company/Group (either in terms of property number or total aggregate borrowing)?
criteria.notes: Only 1 residential mortgage with Accord maximum loan amount £5million for BTL maximum of 3 properties and max lending of £1m with the Group

criteria.criteria_name: Total Aggregate Borrowing on Portfolio with Other Lenders
tags: total aggregate borrowing with other lenders
total loan amount with other lenders
criteria.question: Will the lender consider an application without any Exposure Limits with other lenders (either in terms of property number or total aggregate borrowing)?
criteria.notes: Refer

criteria.question: What Trading History is required to be considered for Affordability and how many Years of Accounts are required?
criteria.notes: Require 2 years and accountants reference, latest personal and business bank statement. If accountant not suitably qualified or no accountant will require 2 years SA302's.

criteria.question: Is a Mileage or a Travel Allowance an acceptable source of income for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: No

criteria.question: Will the lender ignore expenses for Travel in the assessment of affordability?
criteria.notes: Yes. Travel costs are not deducted for the calculation of affordability unless excessive.

criteria.question: How are Tronc Payments treated as a source of income by the lender for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: Not acceptable

criteria.criteria_name: Two Applicants on Mortgage but One Named on Deeds
tags: joint mortgage single name on deeds
two applicants on mortgage one on deeds
one person named on the deeds
criteria.question: Will the lender consider an application from joint borrowers where only one will be the legal proprietor / owner?
criteria.notes: Yes

criteria.question: Are Tyneside Flats / Cross Leases considered to be acceptable security to the Lender?
criteria.notes: Refer

criteria.question: What is the Lender's policy on UK Residents and/or EU Nationals? I.e. Does the Applicant have to be resident in the UK for a certain period of time before an application can be considered?
criteria.notes: Applicants must be resident in the UK at time of application. Applicants from the European Union, European Economic Area and Switzerland will need to provide proof of residency. This excludes Irish nationals. Settled or pre-settled status from the EU settlement scheme will be accepted, with applicants required to provide their government website share code. For both residential and buy-to-let products there will be no LTV restrictions applied to either of the above statuses. Would-be borrowers without settlement status will still be able to apply for a mortgage under Accord's existing criteria for customers without permanent right to reside.

criteria.question: Are Self Employed Applicants who work under an Umbrella Company an acceptable form of employment and source of income for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: Applications may be considered where a two-year track record of this type of income can be demonstrated. A letter from the umbrella company confirming the total amount earned during the last two years and the potential income in the forthcoming year must be obtained.

criteria.question: Will the lender typically allow underpayments?
criteria.notes: No

criteria.question: Are Properties that have been Underpinned ever considered to be acceptable security to the Lender?
criteria.notes: Refer May require structural engineer report.

criteria.question: Will the Lender consider an Application to Mortgage a property that is Unencumbered? I.e. is the Application treated as a Remortgage? And if so, are Free Legals available?
criteria.notes: Yes, remortgage, refer to product literature

criteria.criteria_name: Unit Trusts or OEIC as an Interest Only Repayment Vehicle
criteria.question: Will the Lender accept Unit Trusts or Open Ended Investment Companies (OEICs) as an acceptable Repayment Strategy on an Interest Only Mortgage?
criteria.notes: Acceptable - needs to be sterling UK based and cover 100% of the mortgage.

criteria.question: Is Universal Credit an acceptable source of income for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: Acceptable maximum 60% used, benefit must be sustainable & evidenced

criteria.question: Will the Lender consider an application where there has been any history of any Arrears on an Unsecured Loan?
criteria.notes: For unsecured credit, Worst status of no more than 2 in the last 24 months. In addition to this, the latest recorded payment must have been made. These apply to all insight, including communications, utility, fixed term and mail order.

criteria.criteria_name: Utility Bills Missed or Late Payments
criteria.question: Will the Lender consider an application where there has been any history of Missed or Late Payments or Arrears on Utility Bills?
criteria.notes: For unsecured credit, Worst status of no more than 2 in the last 24 months. In addition to this, the latest recorded payment must have been made. These apply to all insight, including communications, utility, fixed term and mail order.

criteria.question: What is the cut off for the Validity of the latest set of Accounts that are acceptable to be considered for Affordability?
criteria.notes: If the latest set of accounts is over 18 months then we will require a projection for the coming year to ensure the income used is sustainable going forward.

criteria.question: Will the Lender allow the result of a Valuation to be Appealed? If so, what is the policy and process for this? I.e. is there a specific form, how many comparables are needed, in what distance from the security address and at what status etc?
criteria.notes: Valuations can be appealed on a case by case basis. Would need at least 3 comparables to be considered which Accord may forward to the valuer for consideration.

criteria.question: Which Valuation Company is the preferred Valuer for the Mortgage Lender?
criteria.notes: SBVS

criteria.criteria_name: Valuation Fee / Home Survey Fee Scale
criteria.question: What is the lenderâ€™s Valuation Fee Scale?
criteria.notes: Refer to lender for costs

criteria.question: Where a Valuation Fee has been charged upfront, are there any instances in which it will be refunded should the case not proceed?
criteria.notes: Refer to lender

criteria.question: Will the lender instruct a valuation immediately on application?
criteria.notes: Yes

criteria.question: What Types of Valuation is the Applicant able to arrange via the Mortgage Lender?
criteria.notes: Standard valuation, Home buyers Valuation, Full structural report.

criteria.question: Is a Vendor Gifted Deposit acceptable to the Lender?
criteria.notes: Not acceptable.

criteria.question: Is Vested Share Income an acceptable source of income for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: Refer

criteria.criteria_name: Voluntary Right to Buy (Housing Association Tenants)
criteria.question: Will the Lender consider an Application for a Voluntary Right To Buy (Housing Association Tenants)? If so, what is the policy on this?
criteria.notes: No

criteria.question: Is a War Widow(ers) Pension an acceptable source of income for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: 100% acceptable.

criteria.question: Will the Lender allow the mortgage to be set up to allow the customer to make weekly payments instead of monthly? If so, how is this arranged and by who?
criteria.notes: Yes - They will need to contact the payments team on 0345 1200 072 to discuss, payments cannot be made by direct debit weekly but they can send the payments manually each week if they want to.

criteria.question: Is a Widow's Pension an acceptable source of income for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: 100% acceptable.

criteria.question: Is Widowed Parent Allowance an acceptable source of income for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: Up to 100% can considered under appeal.

criteria.criteria_name: Wimpey No Fines / Laing Easi Form post 1945 - Non Standard Construction
criteria.question: Are properties identified as a No Fines or Easi Form Construction and built after 1945 considered suitable security for the Lender? If so, what is the policy on this?
criteria.notes: Usually acceptable subject to valuers comments.

criteria.criteria_name: Wimpey No Fines / Laing Easi Form pre 1945 - Non Standard Construction
criteria.question: Are properties identified as a No Fines or Easi Form Construction and built prior to 1945 considered suitable security for the Lender? If so, what is the policy on this?
criteria.notes: Refer to lender

criteria.question: Will the Lender consider an application from a person who is employed by their own Family's Business?
criteria.notes: We will require an employers reference completed by the company accountant. We will also need payslips and bank statement and the latest p60.

criteria.question: Will the lender consider an applicant who does not have indefinite leave to remain but who does have a Tier 1 Investor/Entrepreneur Working Visa? If so, what is the policy on this?
criteria.notes: Accord Mortgages can consider applicants that hold a 'Tier 1' working visa. No settled/pre-settled status or indefinite leave to remain not granted If at least one applicant has a minimum income of £50,000 the maximum lending LTV is 90%. If no applicants meet the minimum income of £50,000, the maximum lending LTV is 75%.

criteria.question: Does the lender take into account â€˜Worst Statusâ€™ on credit files and if so, what is the worst status that they will accept?
criteria.notes: Secured loans: No more than a worst status in the last 24 months of 1 on a single account Unsecured credit: Worst status of no more than 2 in the last 24 months. In addition to this, the latest recorded payment must have been made. These apply to all insight, including communications, utility, fixed term and mail order.

criteria.question: Are Zero Hours Contracts an acceptable form of employment and source of income for Affordability Assessment?
criteria.notes: 60% of contract income can be used, subject to the applicant working in the following occupations: • NHS Bank nurses and locums • Non-NHS Bank nurses • Care Home Workers • Supermarket Workers • HGV Drivers • Retained / On-Call Firefighters • Armed Forces Reserves • Supply Teachers 12 months evidence of sustainable earning on a zero-hour contract basis is also required
